transcript:
Agent: Thank you for calling SkyWings Airlines. My name is David. How can I help you today?
Customer: Hi David. My flight was cancelled this morning — FL-2847 from Chicago to Miami. I have a business meeting tomorrow that I absolutely cannot miss.
Agent: I sincerely apologize for the disruption. Let me pull up your booking. Can I get your confirmation number?
Customer: It's SKW-771234.
Agent: Thank you. I see your booking. Flight FL-2847 was cancelled due to severe weather at Chicago O'Hare. I want to get you rebooked as quickly as possible. Do you have any flexibility on departure time?
Customer: I need to arrive in Miami by 6 PM today. My meeting is at 9 AM tomorrow but I have a dinner tonight too.
Agent: Understood. Let me check all available options. We have one seat left on FL-2901 departing at 1:15 PM today, arriving Miami at 4:45 PM. That gets you in with time to spare.
Customer: That works. Is there a fee to change?
Agent: Since this is an airline-initiated cancellation, there is absolutely no change fee. I'll move you to FL-2901 right now. Same seat class?
Customer: Yes, business class.
Agent: Confirmed. You're rebooked on FL-2901, business class, departing 1:15 PM. Your new boarding pass will be emailed shortly. Would you also like a meal voucher for the airport?
Customer: That would be appreciated. Also, my checked bag is already tagged for the cancelled flight.
Agent: Your bag will be automatically reticketed to the new flight. No need to recheck. I'm also adding a $15 meal voucher to your boarding pass. You can use it at any airport restaurant in Terminal 2.
Customer: That's very helpful. What gate is the new flight?
Agent: It departs from Gate B22. I'd recommend arriving by 12:30 PM. Is there anything else I can help you with?
Customer: No, that covers everything. Thanks for sorting this out so quickly, David.
Agent: Of course. Safe travels and good luck with your meeting tomorrow!
